data.description:
# Spike: verify Claude Code loads AGENTS.md via a `@AGENTS.md` import

## What

De-risk the whole migration by confirming, hands-on, that a `CLAUDE.md` whose body is a
single `@AGENTS.md` line actually pulls `AGENTS.md` into Claude Code's context at launch
(the mechanism the research identified, but whose live doc page had a fetch bug). Document
the observed precedence/nesting behavior. If the import does NOT work in the installed Claude
Code version, record the fallback decision (symlink `CLAUDE.md -> AGENTS.md`, accepting its
Windows/tool-clobber tradeoffs) so downstream stories build on a proven mechanism.

## Scope

A throwaway spike in a scratch dir or worktree — no durable code change. Files touched:
a temporary `AGENTS.md` + `CLAUDE.md`. Output is a written finding recorded as a comment on
this ticket (and, if needed, a correction to the approach in the parent epic).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Confirmed whether `@AGENTS.md` in `CLAUDE.md` loads `AGENTS.md` into context in the
  installed Claude Code build; result recorded as a comment on this ticket with the exact
  version tested.
  - Proving: comment on this ticket stating PASS/FAIL + `claude --version`.
- [ ] If FAIL, the fallback mechanism (symlink vs. content-copy-with-sync-test) is chosen and
  noted so the AGENTS.md story uses it.
- [ ] No stray spike files left in the repo working tree.
  - Proving: `git status --porcelain` clean after the spike.
data.title: Spike: verify Claude Code loads AGENTS.md via a `@AGENTS.md` import
